commit.message:
Refactor DSP to stereo, fix drum preset export, and misc improvements

- Refactor DSP from mono int16 to interleaved stereo int16 (L,R,L,R,...)
- Mono WAV files duplicated to both channels on load
- Support PCM 16-bit, PCM 24-bit, and IEEE float 32-bit input
- WAV output always stereo 16-bit PCM
- Waveform display and zero-crossing use L channel
- Fix DSP export symbol: plugin_init -> move_plugin_init_v2
- Fix build.sh to copy from src/dsp/dsp.so (Docker output path)
- Fix drum preset sampleUri: URL-encode spaces (%20)
- Default auto slice threshold from 5 to 25
- Disable loop playback in slice mode, restore on exit
- Back from record-ready state exits module entirely

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ego-lint): widen R-VER48-02 guard-window for distant version checks (#53)

## Summary

- Increase R-VER48-02 `guard-window` from 3 to 10 to recognize
PACKAGE_VERSION guards that appear far from the deprecated API call
(e.g., cached in a variable then used in nested ternaries)
- Add test fixture `version-guard-distant@test` reproducing the Dash to
Panel false-positive pattern where the guard is 5-9 lines from the
deprecated `Meta.disable_unredirect_for_display()` call

## Context

In Dash to Panel's `src/utils.js`, the version-compat guard
`Config.PACKAGE_VERSION >= '48'` is cached in a `v48` variable, then
used in nested `if/else` ternaries. The deprecated
`Meta.enable_unredirect_for_display()` and
`Meta.disable_unredirect_for_display()` calls appear 5 and 9 lines after
the guard respectively. With `guard-window: 3`, the sliding `deque`
lookback had already evicted the guard line, causing false positives.

Closes #39
Closes #42

## Test plan

- [x] New fixture `version-guard-distant@test` passes (R-VER48-02
suppressed)
- [x] Existing fixture `version-guard-pkgver@test` still passes
(close-range guard)
- [x] Full test suite passes with zero regressions

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(TEC-179): swap Chat and Tools as primary/secondary navigation (#85)

### What

Reorganized the application's primary navigation and routing structure:

- **Navigation**: Reordered sidebar items to make Chat the primary
destination (first item) and Tools secondary
- **Routing**: Changed the root path (`/`) to render the ChatPage
instead of ToolsCatalogPage
- **Route structure**: Moved ToolsCatalogPage to `/tools` by creating a
new nested route structure (`/tools` layout + `/tools/` index)
- **Chat logic**: Updated the Chat navigation item to treat `/` as the
"new chat" path (previously `/chat`), with logic to prevent state reset
when already in chat

### Why

This change reflects a shift in application priorities, making Chat the
primary user-facing feature and entry point. By moving Tools to a
secondary route, the UX flow is optimized for the chat-first experience.
The routing changes ensure that the root path naturally leads users to
the chat interface while maintaining the Tools catalog at a dedicated
URL.

### Test

- Verified sidebar navigation items render in correct order (Chat first,
Tools second)
- Confirmed root path (`/`) loads ChatPage with proper search parameter
validation (templateId, mandantId)
- Tested that `/tools` route correctly displays ToolsCatalogPage
- Verified Chat navigation item prevents state reset when clicking while
already in chat section
- Confirmed Tools navigation item correctly activates when on `/tools/*`
paths

### Notes

- The chat search schema validates optional `templateId` and `mandantId`
parameters
- Navigation active state checks updated to reflect new route structure
- The comment in the Chat nav item was updated to reflect that `/` (not
`/chat`) now means "new chat"

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase 3 geopolitical crisis event with three choices:

- Ignore the Situation: +legitimacy, -influence

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: re-pick existing UEFA matches that had conf=0 when odds arrive

fetch_uefa_fixtures() now builds a conf0_map of existing pending UEFA
matches with no pick (conf=0). When odds become available for such a
match, it generates a Groq pick and updates the existing entry in-place
(pick, prob, odds_wh) instead of skipping it, then clears aiCtxHash so
generate_analysis.py regenerates the AI text on the next daily run.

Call site updated to pass the full existing upcoming list instead of
just existing_ids, so in-place mutation is visible to main().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: engineering toolkit transformation (Phases A-I)

Add GuardedRobot 1-line API, partenit-eval with grade A-F, SVG reports,
decision replay/record CLI, policy simulator, and Isaac Sim integration.

New tools:
- GuardedRobot — 1-line safety guard wrapping any RobotAdapter
- partenit-eval — scenario evaluation with grade A-F and HTML report
- partenit-log replay / partenit-replay — rich terminal + HTML decision timeline
- partenit-record — session listing and export
- partenit-policy sim — interactive policy simulation (no hardware)
- partenit-scenario — alias for partenit-bench

Eval metrics fixes:
- ai_score = 1 - high_risk_tick_rate (orthogonal to safety_score)
- Absolute-scale collision/near-miss normalizers (independent of run length)
- Baseline unsafe_acceptance_rate now correctly uses tick-level risk heuristic
- EvalReport stores raw ScenarioResult for SVG chart rendering

Report improvements:
- 8 SVG charts per eval: 2D trajectory + distance/speed/risk time-series
- Policy fire timeline with BLOCKED/MODIFIED labels
- Grade badges (A-F) with score breakdown

New files:
- partenit/packages/agent-guard/…/guarded_robot.py
- partenit/packages/safety-bench/…/eval/{__init__,cli,metrics,runner,report_eval}.py
- partenit/packages/adapters/…/isaac_sim_extension/{extension.py,config/extension.toml}
- examples/guarded_robot_demo.py, examples/eval_demo.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add guest company name, date search in bookings, day-by-day checkout invoice, and night audit tax split

- Add company_name field to guest model, CRUD handlers, and all frontend forms (guest config, booking modal, guest selector)
- Add date search filter in bookings page to search by check-in/check-out date
- Show day-by-day room charges in checkout invoice instead of lump sum
- Display guest company name in checkout invoice guest info section
- Separate room charges from service tax in night audit journal using back-calculation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: force dynamic rendering on home page to serve live market data (#76)

The home page was statically pre-rendered at build time, causing all
users to see stale market data after deployment. Adding
`export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` ensures the page is
server-rendered on every request, fetching fresh data from the API.

Closes #73

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: remove linear pipeline, extract shared orchestrator into core.py

The codebase had two execution models (engine.py linear pipeline and
agent_loop.py agentic decomposition) but only the agent path was ever
used. This extracts the 18 shared methods into core.py (~700 lines),
deletes engine.py (2079 lines), removes agent_mode_enabled config flag,
and simplifies app.py routing to always use agent_loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add release workflow safety guards (#1001)

## Summary
- Restrict release triggers to `published` only (removes duplicate
`released`/`edited`/`prereleased`)
- Add `timeout-minutes: 15` to publish job
- Add fail-fast guard when no .nupkg files found
- Add version verification step comparing release tag to package
filenames
- Skip version check on `workflow_dispatch` (no tag available)
- Pass `NUGET_API_KEY` via env variable instead of inline expression
- Use exact version comparison instead of substring regex match

Closes #976

## Review iterations
- Initial implementation: 4 safety guards
- Review found 5 issues (duplicate trigger, broken workflow_dispatch,
empty package false-pass, loose version match, secret interpolation)
- All 5 fixed and re-reviewed to PASS

## Test plan
- [x] YAML syntax verified
- [x] PowerShell variable scoping correct
- [x] Version extraction handles `Moq.Analyzers.X.Y.Z` naming

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Chores**
  * Streamlined release automation and tightened release triggers.
  * Added a 15-minute timeout for the publish job to prevent hung runs.
* Added package version verification to ensure published packages match
release tags.
* Improved NuGet publishing reliability with authentication, retries,
backoff and detailed publish logging.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

fix(search): read query param client-side to fix broken search on static deploy

In output:static mode, Astro.url.searchParams is always empty at build time,
so defaultValue was always '' and the auto-search never triggered.
Switched to is:inline script that reads window.location.search at runtime.
Also fixed a DOMContentLoaded race condition with a readyState guard.

commit.message:
Adopt lick extraction v2 (LBDM+MLA+DTL) and document algorithm in detail

- Run extract_licks_v2.py: LBDM phrase boundary detection (Cambouropoulos 2001),
  MLA lick/line classification (Frieler/Pfleiderer 2016), DTL pattern frequency
  scoring via Levenshtein clustering. ~11,000 licks from 236 solos.
- Rewrite docs/index.html Lick Library section with full v2 algorithm documentation:
  8-step pipeline, LBDM formula explanation, MLA criteria, DTL scoring, statistics
- Update CLAUDE.md: file structure (v1/v2), lick count (~11k), library size (~2.1MB)
